title: rosa viscaria var. septicola
intro: Rosa viscaria var. septicola, also known as the alpenrose or Alpine rose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the European Alps. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Rosa viscaria var. septicola include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Rosa viscaria var. septicola can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or consumed orally to reap its benefits.
